resources.string: Velvet hides the barbs; with every caress, the self forgets its boundaries.

Silk drinks sweat and blood until both gleam as one. Familiar whispers brush against the ear, soft enough to trust, sharp enough to cut. Every touch strikes like lightning. Pain and pleasure trade names, then lose them altogether. At the summit of delight, the soul is not held; it shatters into sensation, and every fragment thanks the abyss for its embrace.
